mnemonics: BIT-BAG: Bits In The Box Are Grouped - A reminder that framing involves grouping bits into manageable blocks for transmission.
keyPoints: Framing is the process of delineating a sequence of bits into discrete, manageable blocks or frames for data link layer transmission.
Common framing methods include character (byte) counting, character stuffing, and bit stuffing, each with mechanisms to handle special flag sequences.
The frame structure typically includes a header (with flags and control info), a payload (the actual data), and a trailer (often with error detection like CRC).
commonMistakes: Confusing framing (Data Link Layer) with segmentation (Transport Layer); framing deals with physical bit streams, while segmentation deals with breaking down larger data units.
Forgetting that the primary purpose of framing is not just organization but to enable error control, flow control, and reliable delivery by creating manageable data units.
